    "name": "CONTRIBUTING.md",
    "path": "CONTRIBUTING.md",
    "type": "file",
    "content": "# Contributing\n\nThank you for considering making contributions to the OWASP Amass Project! Start by joining our [Discord server](https://discord.gg/ANTyEDUXt5), taking a look at the [Documentation](https://owasp-amass.github.io/docs/), and checking out the [open issues](https://github.com/owasp-amass/amass/issues) for things we need help with!\n\nPlease follow standard github best practices: fork the repo, branch from the tip of develop, make some commits, and submit a pull request to develop. \n\nPlease make sure to use `gofmt` before every commit - the easiest way to do this is have your editor run it for you upon saving a file. Otherwise, run the following command in the project root directory: `go fmt ./...`\n\nIt is recommended that you use the Golang Linter before every commit, to help you catch errors and to write clean code: `golangci-lint run ./...`\n\n## Forking\n\nPlease note that Go requires code to live under absolute paths, which complicates forking.\nWhile the fork for user 'foo' lives at `https://github.com/foo/amass`,\nthe code should never exist at  `$GOPATH/src/github.com/foo/amass`.\nInstead, we use `git remote` to add the fork as a new remote for the original repo,\n`$GOPATH/src/github.com/owasp-amass/amass`, and do all the work there.\n\nFor instance, to create a fork and work on a branch of it, user 'foo' would:\n\n  * Create the fork on github, using the fork button.\n  * Go to the original repo checked out locally (ie. `$GOPATH/src/github.com/owasp-amass/amass`)\n  * `git remote rename origin upstream`\n  * `git remote add origin git@github.com:foo/amass.git`\n\nNow `origin` refers to the foo fork and `upstream` refers to the OWASP version.\nSo foo can `git push -u origin develop` to update his/her fork, and make pull requests to OWASP from there.\nOf course, replace `foo` with your git handle.\n\nTo pull in updates from the original repo, run\n\n    * `git fetch upstream`\n    * `git rebase upstream/main` (or whatever branch you want)\n\nPlease do not make pull requests to `main`.\n\n### Development Procedure:\n- the latest state of development is on `develop`\n- no --force onto `develop` (except when reverting a broken commit, which should seldom happen)\n- create a development branch on your fork (using `git add origin`)\n- before submitting a pull request, begin `git rebase` on top of `develop`\n"
  },
  {
    "name": "Dockerfile",
    "path": "Dockerfile",
    "type": "file",
    "content": "FROM golang:1.24.4-alpine AS build\nRUN apk --no-cache add git\nWORKDIR /go/src/github.com/owasp-amass/amass\nCOPY . .\nRUN CGO_ENABLED=0 go install -v ./...\n\nFROM alpine:latest\nRUN apk add --no-cache bash ca-certificates\nRUN apk --no-cache --update upgrade\nCOPY --from=build /go/bin/amass /bin/amass\nCOPY --from=build /go/bin/oam_enum /bin/enum\nCOPY --from=build /go/bin/amass_engine /bin/engine\nCOPY --from=build /go/bin/ae_isready /bin/ae_isready\nCOPY --from=build /go/bin/oam_subs /bin/subs\nCOPY --from=build /go/bin/oam_assoc /bin/assoc\nCOPY --from=build /go/bin/oam_viz /bin/viz\nCOPY --from=build /go/bin/oam_track /bin/track\nCOPY --from=build /go/bin/oam_i2y /bin/i2y\nENV HOME=/\nRUN addgroup user \\\n    && adduser user -D -G user \\\n    && mkdir /.config \\\n    && chown user:user /.config \\\n    && mkdir /.config/amass \\\n    && chown user:user /.config/amass \\\n    && mkdir /data \\\n    && chown user:user /data\nUSER user\nWORKDIR /data\nSTOPSIGNAL SIGINT\nENTRYPOINT [\"/bin/amass\"]"

    "content": "# [![OWASP Logo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/owasp-amass/docs/master/images/owasp_logo.png) OWASP Amass Project](https://owasp.org/www-project-amass/)\n\n[![OWASP Flagship](https://img.shields.io/badge/owasp-flagship%20project-48A646.svg)](https://owasp.org/projects/#sec-flagships)\n[![GitHub Release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/owasp-amass/amass)](https://github.com/owasp-amass/amass/releases/latest)\n[![Go Report](https://goreportcard.com/badge/github.com/owasp-amass/amass)](https://goreportcard.com/report/github.com/owasp-amass/amass)\n[![CodeFactor](https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/owasp-amass/amass/badge)](https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/owasp-amass/amass)\n[![Follow on Twitter](https://img.shields.io/twitter/follow/owaspamass.svg?logo=twitter)](https://twitter.com/owaspamass)\n[![Chat on Discord](https://img.shields.io/discord/433729817918308352.svg?logo=discord)](https://discord.gg/ANTyEDUXt5)\n\nThe OWASP Amass Project performs network mapping of attack surfaces and external asset discovery using open source information gathering and active reconnaissance techniques.\n\n## Installation [![Go Version](https://img.shields.io/github/go-mod/go-version/owasp-amass/amass)](https://golang.org/dl/) [![Docker Image](https://img.shields.io/docker/pulls/owaspamass/amass.svg)](https://hub.docker.com/r/owaspamass/amass) [![GitHub Downloads](https://img.shields.io/github/downloads/owasp-amass/amass/latest/total.svg)](https://github.com/owasp-amass/amass/releases/latest)\n\n> You can find additional installation instructions and documentation in the [Amass Docs](https://owasp-amass.github.io/docs) repo.\n\n## Corporate Supporters\n\n[![WhoisXML API Logo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/owasp-amass/docs/master/images/supporters/whoisxmlapi_logo.png)](https://www.whoisxmlapi.com/)\n\n## Contributing [![Contribute Yes](https://img.shields.io/badge/contribute-yes-brightgreen.svg)](./CONTRIBUTING.md) [![Chat on Discord](https://img.shields.io/discord/433729817918308352.svg?logo=discord)](https://discord.gg/ANTyEDUXt5)\n\nWe are always happy to get new contributors on board! \"github.com/owasp-amass/amass/v5/internal/amass_engine\"\n\t\"github.com/owasp-amass/amass/v5/internal/assoc\"\n\t\"github.com/owasp-amass/amass/v5/internal/enum\"\n\t\"github.com/owasp-amass/amass/v5/internal/subs\"\n\t\"github.com/owasp-amass/amass/v5/internal/tools\"\n\t\"github.com/owasp-amass/amass/v5/internal/track\"\n\t\"github.com/owasp-amass/amass/v5/internal/viz\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\tusageMsg string = \"[assoc|engine|enum|subs|track|viz] [options]\"\n)\n\ntype Args struct {\n\tHelp    bool\n\tVersion bool\n}\n\ntype subDesc struct {\n\tName        string\n\tDescription string\n}\n\nvar subcommands = []subDesc{\n\t{\"assoc\", assoc.Description},\n\t{\"engine\", ae.Description},\n\t{\"enum\", enum.Description},\n\t{\"subs\", subs.Description},\n\t{\"track\", track.Description},\n\t{\"viz\", viz.Description},\n}\n\nfunc main() {\n\tvar args Args\n\tfs := flag.NewFlagSet(\"amass\", flag.ContinueOnError)\n\n\tfs.BoolVar(&args.Help, \"h\", false, \"Show the program usage message\")\n\tfs.BoolVar(&args.Help, \"help\", false, \"Show the program usage message\")\n\tfs.BoolVar(&args.Version, \"version\", false, \"Print the Amass version number\")\n\n\tdefaultBuf := new(bytes.Buffer)\n\tfs.SetOutput(defaultBuf)\n\n\tvar usage = func() {\n\t\tafmt.PrintBanner()\n\t\t_, _ = afmt.G.Fprintf(color.Error, \"Usage: %s %s\\n\\n\", path.Base(os.Args[0]), usageMsg)\n\n\t\tif args.Help {\n\t\t\tfs.PrintDefaults()\n\t\t\t_, _ = afmt.G.Fprintln(color.Error, defaultBuf.String())\n\t\t\t_, _ = afmt.G.Fprintf(color.Error, \"Subcommands: \\n\\n\")\n\t\t\tfor _, sub := range subcommands {\n\t\t\t\t_, _ = afmt.G.Fprintf(color.Error, \"\\t%-5s\\t%s\\n\", sub.Name, sub.Description)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t_, _ = afmt.G.Fprintln(color.Error)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t_, _ = afmt.G.Fprintln(color.Error, \"Use the -h or --help flag to see the flags and subcommands\")\n\t\t_, _ = afmt.G.Fprintf(color.Error, \"\\nThe Amass Discord server can be found here: %s\\n\\n\", afmt.DiscordInvitation)\n\t}\n\n\tif len(os.Args) < 2 {\n\t\tusage()\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif err := fs.Parse(os.Args[1:]); err != nil {\n\t\tusage()\n\t\t_, _ = afmt.R.Fprintf(color.Error, \"%v\\n\", err)\n\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t}\n\tif args.Help {\n\t\tusage()\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif args.Version {\n\t\t_, _ = afmt.G.Fprintf(color.Error, \"%s\\n\", afmt.Version)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Ensure the output directory exists\n\tif err := tools.CreateOutputDirectory(\"\"); err != nil {\n\t\t_, _ = afmt.R.Fprintf(color.Error, \"Failed to create the output directory: %v\\n\", err)\n\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t}\n\n\tdir := config.OutputDirectory(\"\")\n\tif dir == \"\" {\n\t\t_, _ = afmt.R.Fprintln(color.Error, \"failed to obtain the path for the output directory\")\n\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t}\n\n\t// Ensure the default config files exist\n\tif err := tools.CreateDefaultConfigFiles(dir); err != nil {\n\t\t_, _ = afmt.R.Fprintf(color.Error, \"Failed to create the default config files: %v\\n\", err)\n\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t}\n\n\tcmdName := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s\", path.Base(os.Args[0]), os.Args[1])\n\tswitch os.Args[1] {\n\tcase \"assoc\":\n\t\tassoc.CLIWorkflow(cmdName, os.Args[2:])\n\tcase \"engine\":\n\t\tif engineIsRunning() {\n\t\t\t_, _ = afmt.R.Fprintf(color.Error, \"The Amass engine is already running.\\n\")\n\t\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tae.CLIWorkflow(cmdName, os.Args[2:])\n\tcase \"enum\":\n\t\t// The engine must be started before running the enum command\n\t\tif !engineIsRunning() {\n\t\t\tif err := startEngine(); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t_, _ = afmt.R.Fprintf(color.Error, \"Failed to start the Amass engine: %v\\n\", err)\n\t\t\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Give the engine time to start\n\t\t\tif err := waitForEngineResponse(); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t_, _ = afmt.R.Fprintf(color.Error, \"The Amass engine did not respond: %v\\n\", err)\n\t\t\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tenum.CLIWorkflow(cmdName, os.Args[2:])\n\tcase \"subs\":\n\t\tsubs.CLIWorkflow(cmdName, os.Args[2:])\n\tcase \"track\":\n\t\ttrack.CLIWorkflow(cmdName, os.Args[2:])\n\tcase \"viz\":\n\t\tviz.CLIWorkflow(cmdName, os.Args[2:])\n\tdefault:\n\t\tusage()\n\t\t_, _ = afmt.R.Fprintf(color.Error, \"subcommand provided but not defined: %s\\n\", os.Args[1])\n\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc waitForEngineResponse() error {\n\tt := time.NewTicker(time.Second)\n\tdefer t.Stop()\n\n\tfor range 60 {\n\t\t<-t.C\n\t\tif engineIsRunning() {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"the Amass engine did not respond within the timeout period\")\n}\n"

All rights reserved.\n// Use of this source code is governed by Apache 2 LICENSE that can be found in the LICENSE file.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\npackage main\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net\"\n\t\"regexp\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\n\t\"github.com/caffix/stringset\"\n\t\"github.com/go-ini/ini\"\n\t\"github.com/owasp-amass/amass/v5/internal/net/dns\"\n)\n\n// Updater allows an object to implement a method that updates a configuration.\ntype Updater interface {\n\tOverrideConfig(*Config) error\n}\n\n// Config passes along Amass configuration settings and options.\ntype Config struct {\n\tsync.Mutex\n\n\t// The graph databases used by the system / enumerations\n\tGraphDBs []*Database\n\n\t// The IP addresses specified as in scope\n\tAddresses []string\n\n\t// CIDR that is in scope\n\tCIDRs []string\n\n\t// ASNs specified as in scope\n\tASNs []int\n\n\t// The ports that will be checked for certificates\n\tPorts []int\n\n\t// Will the enumeration including brute forcing techniques\n\tBruteForcing bool\n\n\tBruteforcelist []string\n\n\t// Will recursive brute forcing be performed?\n\tRecursive bool\n\n\t// Will discovered subdomain name alterations be generated?\n\tAlterations bool\n\n\tAlterationslist []string\n\n\t// Only access the data sources for names and return results?\n\tPassive bool\n\n\t// Determines if zone transfers will be attempted\n\tActive bool\n\n\t// A blacklist of subdomain names that will not be investigated\n\tBlacklist []string\n\n\t// The minimum number of minutes that data source responses will be reused\n\tMinimumTTL int\n\n\t// Resolver settings\n\tResolvers []string\n\n\t// The root domain names that the enumeration will target\n\tdomains []string\n\n\t// The regular expressions for the root domains added to the enumeration\n\tregexps map[string]*regexp.Regexp\n\n\t// The data source configurations\n\tdatasrcConfigs map[string]*DataSourceConfig\n}\n\n// DataSourceConfig contains the configurations specific to a data source.\ntype DataSourceConfig struct {\n\tName  string\n\tTTL   int `ini:\"ttl\"`\n\tcreds map[string]*Credentials\n}\n\n// Credentials contains values required for authenticating with web APIs.\ntype Credentials struct {\n\tName     string\n\tUsername string `ini:\"username\"`\n\tPassword string `ini:\"password\"`\n\tKey      string `ini:\"apikey\"`\n\tSecret   string `ini:\"secret\"`\n}\n\n// Database contains values required for connecting with graph databases.\ntype Database struct {\n\tSystem   string\n\tPrimary  bool   `ini:\"primary\"`\n\tURL      string `ini:\"url\"`\n\tUsername string `ini:\"username\"`\n\tPassword string `ini:\"password\"`\n\tDBName   string `ini:\"database\"`\n\tOptions  string `ini:\"options\"`\n}\n\n// LoadSettings parses settings from an .ini file and assigns them to the Config.\nfunc (c *Config) LoadSettings(path string) error {\n\tcfg, err := ini.LoadSources(ini.LoadOptions{\n\t\tInsensitive:  true,\n\t\tAllowShadows: true,\n\t}, path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to load the configuration file: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\t// Get the easy ones out of the way using mapping\n\tif err = cfg.MapTo(c); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error mapping configuration settings to internal values: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\t// Attempt to load a special mode of operation specified by the user\n\tif cfg.Section(ini.DefaultSection).HasKey(\"mode\") {\n\t\tmode := cfg.Section(ini.DefaultSection).Key(\"mode\").String()\n\n\t\tswitch mode {\n\t\tcase \"passive\":\n\t\t\tc.Passive = true\n\t\tcase \"active\":\n\t\t\tc.Active = true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tloads := []func(cfg *ini.File) error{\n\t\tc.loadResolverSettings,\n\t\tc.loadScopeSettings,\n\t\tc.loadAlterationSettings,\n\t\tc.loadBruteForceSettings,\n\t\tc.loadDatabaseSettings,\n\t\tc.loadDataSourceSettings,\n\t}\n\tfor _, load := range loads {\n\t\tif err := load(cfg); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Config) loadDataSourceSettings(cfg *ini.File) error {\n\tsec, err := cfg.GetSection(\"data_sources\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tif sec.HasKey(\"minimum_ttl\") {\n\t\tif ttl, err := sec.Key(\"minimum_ttl\").Int(); err == nil {\n\t\t\tc.MinimumTTL = ttl\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, child := range sec.ChildSections() {\n\t\tname := strings.Split(child.Name(), \".\")[1]\n\n\t\tif name == \"disabled\" {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdsc := c.GetDataSourceConfig(name)\n\t\t// Parse the Database information and assign to the Config\n\t\tif err := child.MapTo(dsc); err != nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif c.MinimumTTL > dsc.TTL {\n\t\t\tdsc.TTL = c.MinimumTTL\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Check for data source credentials\n\t\tfor _, cr := range child.ChildSections() {\n\t\t\tsetName := strings.Split(cr.Name(), \".\")[2]\n\n\t\t\tcreds := &Credentials{Name: setName}\n\t\t\tif err := cr.MapTo(creds); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif err := dsc.AddCredentials(creds); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Config) loadDatabaseSettings(cfg *ini.File) error {\n\tsec, err := cfg.GetSection(\"graphdbs\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, child := range sec.ChildSections() {\n\t\tdb := new(Database)\n\t\tname := strings.Split(child.Name(), \".\")[1]\n\n\t\t// Parse the Database information and assign to the Config\n\t\tif err := child.MapTo(db); err == nil {\n\t\t\tdb.System = name\n\t\t\tc.GraphDBs = append(c.GraphDBs, db)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Config) loadResolverSettings(cfg *ini.File) error {\n\tsec, err := cfg.GetSection(\"resolvers\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tc.Resolvers = stringset.Deduplicate(sec.Key(\"resolver\").ValueWithShadows())\n\tif len(c.Resolvers) == 0 {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"no resolver keys were found in the resolvers section\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Config) loadScopeSettings(cfg *ini.File) error {\n\tscope, err := cfg.GetSection(\"scope\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tif scope.HasKey(\"address\") {\n\t\tc.Addresses = append(c.Addresses, scope.Key(\"address\").ValueWithShadows()...)\n\t}\n\n\tif scope.HasKey(\"cidr\") {\n\t\tfor _, cidr := range scope.Key(\"cidr\").ValueWithShadows() {\n\t\t\t// Verify the CIDR is valid\n\t\t\tif _, _, err := net.ParseCIDR(cidr); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tc.CIDRs = append(c.CIDRs, cidr)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif scope.HasKey(\"asn\") {\n\t\tfor _, asn := range scope.Key(\"asn\").ValueWithShadows() {\n\t\t\tc.ASNs = uniqueIntAppend(c.ASNs, asn)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif scope.HasKey(\"port\") {\n\t\tfor _, port := range scope.Key(\"port\").ValueWithShadows() {\n\t\t\tc.Ports = uniqueIntAppend(c.Ports, port)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Load up all the DNS domain names\n\tif domains, err := cfg.GetSection(\"scope.domains\"); err == nil {\n\t\tfor _, domain := range domains.Key(\"domain\").ValueWithShadows() {\n\t\t\tc.AddDomain(domain)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Load up all the blacklisted subdomain names\n\tif blacklisted, err := cfg.GetSection(\"scope.blacklisted\"); err == nil {\n\t\tc.Blacklist = stringset.Deduplicate(blacklisted.Key(\"subdomain\").ValueWithShadows())\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Config) loadBruteForceSettings(cfg *ini.File) error {\n\tbruteforce, err := cfg.GetSection(\"bruteforce\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// get the list\n\tc.Bruteforcelist = bruteforce.Key(\"wordlist_file\").ValueWithShadows()\n\n\t// get the options like the worldlist somehow\n\tc.BruteForcing = bruteforce.Key(\"enabled\").MustBool(true)\n\tif !c.BruteForcing {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Config) loadAlterationSettings(cfg *ini.File) error {\n\talterations, err := cfg.GetSection(\"alterations\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// get the list\n\tc.Alterationslist = alterations.Key(\"wordlist_file\").ValueWithShadows()\n\n\tc.Alterations = alterations.Key(\"enabled\").MustBool(true)\n\tif !c.Alterations {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// GetDataSourceConfig returns the DataSourceConfig associated with the data source name argument.\nfunc (c *Config) GetDataSourceConfig(source string) *DataSourceConfig {\n\tc.Lock()\n\tdefer c.Unlock()\n\n\tkey := strings.TrimSpace(source)\n\tif key == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tif c.datasrcConfigs == nil {\n\t\tc.datasrcConfigs = make(map[string]*DataSourceConfig)\n\t}\n\tif _, found := c.datasrcConfigs[key]; !found {\n\t\tc.datasrcConfigs[key] = &DataSourceConfig{Name: key}\n\t}\n\treturn c.datasrcConfigs[key]\n}\n\n// AddCredentials adds the Credentials provided to the configuration.\nfunc (dsc *DataSourceConfig) AddCredentials(cred *Credentials) error {\n\tif cred == nil || cred.Name == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"AddCredentials: The Credentials argument is invalid\")\n\t}\n\n\tif dsc.creds == nil {\n\t\tdsc.creds = make(map[string]*Credentials)\n\t}\n\n\tdsc.creds[cred.Name] = cred\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// AddDomain appends the domain name provided in the parameter to the list in the configuration.\nfunc (c *Config) AddDomain(domain string) {\n\tc.Lock()\n\tdefer c.Unlock()\n\n\t// Check that the domain string is not empty\n\td := strings.TrimSpace(domain)\n\tif d == \"\" {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\t// Check that it is a domain with at least two labels\n\tlabels := strings.Split(d, \".\")\n\tif len(labels) < 2 {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Check that none of the labels are empty\n\tfor _, label := range labels {\n\t\tif label == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Check that the regular expression map has been initialized\n\tif c.regexps == nil {\n\t\tc.regexps = make(map[string]*regexp.Regexp)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create the regular expression for this domain\n\tc.regexps[d] = dns.SubdomainRegex(d)\n\tif c.regexps[d] != nil {\n\t\t// Add the domain string to the list\n\t\tc.domains = append(c.domains, d)\n\t}\n\n\tc.domains = stringset.Deduplicate(c.domains)\n}\n\nfunc uniqueIntAppend(s []int, e string) []int {\n\tif a1, err := strconv.Atoi(e); err == nil {\n\t\tvar found bool\n\n\t\tfor _, a2 := range s {\n\t\t\tif a1 == a2 {\n\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\ts = append(s, a1)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn s\n}\n"
          },

|\n//\t|  #@          :@W    &@+  &@+   @8  :@o       o@o     oW@@W+        oW@8    |\n//\t|  o@+          @@&   &@+  &@+   #@  &@.      .W@W       .+#@&         o@W.  |\n//\t|   WW         +@W@8. &@+ .o#@@W&.   :W@WWW@@&   |\n//\t|      +o&&&&+.                                                    +oooo.    |\n//\t+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\npackage main\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"flag\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"path\"\n\t\"path/filepath\"\n\n\t\"github.com/fatih/color\"\n\t\"github.com/owasp-amass/amass/v5/config\"\n\t\"gopkg.in/yaml.v3\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\tusageMsg = \"[options]\"\n)\n\nvar (\n\tg = color.New(color.FgHiGreen)\n\tr = color.New(color.FgHiRed)\n\tb = color.New(color.FgHiBlue)\n\tp = color.New(color.FgHiMagenta)\n)\n\nfunc main() {\n\tvar help1, help2 bool\n\tvar iniFile, configFile, datasrcFile string\n\tvar err error\n\ti2yCommand := flag.NewFlagSet(\"db\", flag.ContinueOnError)\n\n\ti2yBuf := new(bytes.Buffer)\n\ti2yCommand.SetOutput(i2yBuf)\n\n\ti2yCommand.BoolVar(&help1, \"h\", false, \"Show the program usage message\")\n\ti2yCommand.BoolVar(&help2, \"help\", false, \"Show the program usage message\")\n\ti2yCommand.StringVar(&iniFile, \"ini\", \"\", \"Path to the INI configuration file.\")\n\ti2yCommand.StringVar(&configFile, \"cf\", \"oam_config.yaml\", \"YAML configuration file name.\")\n\ti2yCommand.StringVar(&datasrcFile, \"df\", \"oam_datasources.yaml\", \"YAML data sources file name.\")\n\n\tvar usage = func() {\n\t\t_, _ = g.Fprintf(color.Error, \"Usage: %s %s\\n\\n\", path.Base(os.Args[0]), usageMsg)\n\t\ti2yCommand.PrintDefaults()\n\t\t_, _ = g.Fprintln(color.Error, i2yBuf.String())\n\t}\n\n\tif len(os.Args) < 2 {\n\t\tusage()\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif err := i2yCommand.Parse(os.Args[1:]); err != nil {\n\t\t_, _ = r.Fprintf(color.Error, \"%v\\n\", err)\n\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t}\n\tif help1 || help2 {\n\t\tusage()\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif iniFile == \"\" {\n\t\tusage()\n\t\t_, _ = r.Fprintln(color.Error, \"Failed to parse the INI file: File not present, got \\\"\"+iniFile+\"\\\" as the path.\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// converts the file paths to absolute paths\n\tconfigFile, err = filepath.Abs(configFile)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Fatal(\"Failed to get the absolute config path:\", err)\n\t}\n\tdatasrcFile, err = filepath.Abs(datasrcFile)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Fatal(\"Failed to get the absolute data source path:\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiniConfig := Config{}\n\tif err := iniConfig.LoadSettings(iniFile); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Fatal(\"Failed to load the INI file:\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// this code below will take all the datasources specified in the ini and populate the filled ones into the yaml\n\tyamlDataSources := make([]*config.DataSource, 0)\n\n\tfor _, v := range iniConfig.datasrcConfigs {\n\t\tif len(v.creds) == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue // Skip to the next iteration if there are no credentials\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tcreds := make(map[string]*config.Credentials)\n\t\tfor credKey, credValue := range v.creds {\n\t\t\tcreds[credKey] = &config.Credentials{\n\t\t\t\tName:     credValue.Name,\n\t\t\t\tUsername: credValue.Username,\n\t\t\t\tPassword: credValue.Password,\n\t\t\t\tApikey:   credValue.Key,\n\t\t\t\tSecret:   credValue.Secret,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tyamlDataSources = append(yamlDataSources, &config.DataSource{\n\t\t\tName:  v.Name,\n\t\t\tTTL:   v.TTL,\n\t\t\tCreds: creds,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\t// this part of the code will populate the options only if theyre populated in the ini\n\toptions := make(map[string]interface{})\n\n\tif len(iniConfig.Resolvers) > 0 {\n\t\toptions[\"resolvers\"] = iniConfig.Resolvers\n\t}\n\n\tif iniConfig.BruteForcing {\n\t\tbruteforce := make(map[string]interface{})\n\t\tbruteforce[\"enabled\"] = iniConfig.BruteForcing\n\n\t\tif len(iniConfig.Bruteforcelist) > 0 {\n\t\t\tbruteforce[\"wordlist\"] = iniConfig.Bruteforcelist\n\t\t}\n\n\t\toptions[\"bruteforce\"] = bruteforce\n\t}\n\n\tif iniConfig.Alterations {\n\t\talterations := make(map[string]interface{})\n\t\talterations[\"enabled\"] = iniConfig.Alterations\n\n\t\tif len(iniConfig.Alterationslist) > 0 {\n\t\t\talterations[\"wordlist\"] = iniConfig.Alterationslist\n\t\t}\n\n\t\toptions[\"alterations\"] = alterations\n\t}\n\n\t// this part of the code initializes the yamlconfig file with the values\n\tyamlConfig := config.Config{\n\t\tScope: &config.Scope{\n\t\t\tDomains:     iniConfig.domains,\n\t\t\tIP:          iniConfig.Addresses,\n\t\t\tASNs:        iniConfig.ASNs,\n\t\t\tCIDRStrings: iniConfig.CIDRs,\n\t\t\tPorts:       iniConfig.Ports,\n\t\t\tBlacklist:   iniConfig.Blacklist,\n\t\t},\n\t\tOptions: options,\n\t}\n\n\t// if the databse is present in the ini, then store the first url.\n\tif len(iniConfig.GraphDBs) > 0 {\n\t\tyamlConfig.Options[\"database\"] = iniConfig.GraphDBs[0].URL\n\t}\n\n\t// this part of the code initializes the yamlDataSrcConfigs file with the values\n\tyamlDataSrcConfigs := config.DataSourceConfig{\n\t\tDatasources: yamlDataSources,\n\t\tGlobalOptions: map[string]int{\n\t\t\t\"minimum_ttl\": iniConfig.MinimumTTL,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\t// marshals and outputs it into a file\n\n\toutput, err := yaml.Marshal(yamlDataSrcConfigs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Println(\"failed to marshal the yaml:\", err)\n\t} else {\n\t\tyamlConfig.Options[\"datasources\"] = datasrcFile\n\t\terr = os.WriteFile(datasrcFile, output, 0644)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Println(\"Failed to write datasources file:\", err)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t_, _ = fmt.Println(b.Sprint(\"Wrote data sources file successfully at: \") + p.Sprint(datasrcFile))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\toutput, err = yaml.Marshal(&yamlConfig)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Println(\"failed to marshal the yaml:\", err)\n\t} else {\n\t\terr = os.WriteFile(configFile, output, 0644)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Println(\"Failed to write config file:\", err)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t_, _ = fmt.Println(b.Sprint(\"Wrote config file successfully at \") + p.Sprint(configFile))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n"

= []byte(`\noptions:\n   active: \"true\"\n`)\n\nfunc TestActive(t *testing.T) {\n\tc := NewConfig()\n\n\terr := yaml.Unmarshal(validyaml, c)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Unexpected error: %v\",         "content": "// Copyright © by Jeff Foley 2017-2025. All rights reserved.\n// Use of this source code is governed by Apache 2 LICENSE that can be found in the LICENSE file.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\npackage config\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\t\"github.com/caffix/stringset\"\n)\n\nfunc (c *Config) loadBruteForceSettings(cfg *Config) error {\n\tbruteforceRaw, ok := c.Options[\"bruteforce\"]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tbruteforce, ok := bruteforceRaw.(map[string]interface{})\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"bruteforce is not a map[string]interface{}\")\n\t}\n\n\tenabled, ok := bruteforce[\"enabled\"].(bool)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"bruteforce enabled is not a bool\")\n\t}\n\n\tc.BruteForcing = enabled\n\tif !c.BruteForcing {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tif wordlistPathRaw, ok := bruteforce[\"wordlists\"]; ok {\n\t\twordlistPaths, ok := wordlistPathRaw.([]interface{})\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"bruteforce wordlist_file is not an array\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, wordlistPathRaw := range wordlistPaths {\n\t\t\twordlistPath, ok := wordlistPathRaw.(string)\n\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"bruteforce wordlist_file item is not a string\")\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tabsPath, err := c.AbsPathFromConfigDir(wordlistPath)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get absolute path for wordlist file: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\twordlist, err := GetListFromFile(absPath)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to load the file in the bruteforce wordlist_file setting: %s: %v\", absPath, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tc.Wordlist = append(c.Wordlist, wordlist...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tc.Wordlist = stringset.Deduplicate(c.Wordlist)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Config) loadAlterationSettings(cfg *Config) error {\n\talterationsRaw, ok := c.Options[\"alterations\"]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\talterations, ok := alterationsRaw.(map[string]interface{})\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"alterations is not a map[string]interface{}\")\n\t}\n\n\tenabled, ok := alterations[\"enabled\"].(bool)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"alterations enabled is not a bool\")\n\t}\n\n\tc.Alterations = enabled\n\tif !c.Alterations {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tif wordlistPathRaw, ok := alterations[\"wordlists\"]; ok {\n\t\twordlistPaths, ok := wordlistPathRaw.([]interface{})\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"alterations wordlist_file is not an array\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, wordlistPathRaw := range wordlistPaths {\n\t\t\twordlistPath, ok := wordlistPathRaw.(string)\n\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"alterations wordlist_file item is not a string\")\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tabsPath, err := c.AbsPathFromConfigDir(wordlistPath)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get absolute path for wordlist file: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\twordlist, err := GetListFromFile(absPath)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to load the file in the alterations wordlist_file setting: %s: %v\", absPath, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tc.AltWordlist = append(c.AltWordlist, wordlist...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tc.AltWordlist = stringset.Deduplicate(c.AltWordlist)\n\treturn nil\n}\n"

All rights reserved.\n// Use of this source code is governed by Apache 2 LICENSE that can be found in the LICENSE file.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\npackage config\n\nimport (\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"gopkg.in/yaml.v3\"\n)\n\nfunc TestConfigLoadBruteForceSettings(t *testing.T) {\n\ttype args struct {\n\t\tcfg []byte\n\t}\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname          string\n\t\targs          args\n\t\twantErr       bool\n\t\tassertionFunc func(*testing.T, *Config)\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"success - brute force enabled\",\n\t\t\targs: args{cfg: []byte(`\noptions:\n  brute_force:\n    enabled: true\n    wordlists: []`)},\n\t\t\twantErr: false,\n\t\t\tassertionFunc: func(t *testing.T, c *Config) {\n\t\t\t\tbruteForce, ok := c.Options[\"brute_force\"].(map[string]interface{})\n\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"BruteForce not found\")\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tenabled, ok := bruteForce[\"enabled\"].(bool)\n\t\t\t\tif !ok || !enabled {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"BruteForce not enabled\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\twordlist, ok := bruteForce[\"wordlists\"].([]interface{})\n\t\t\t\tif !ok || len(wordlist) != 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Wordlist not correctly set\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"failure - missing brute force settings\",\n\t\t\targs: args{cfg: []byte(`\noptions:\n  something_else:\n    some_setting: true`)},\n\t\t\twantErr: false,\n\t\t\tassertionFunc: func(t *testing.T, c *Config) {\n\t\t\t\tif _, ok := c.Options[\"brute_force\"]; ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"BruteForce should not be found\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"success - brute force disabled\",\n\t\t\targs: args{cfg: []byte(`\noptions:\n  brute_force:\n    enabled: false\n    wordlists: []`)},\n\t\t\twantErr: false,\n\t\t\tassertionFunc: func(t *testing.T, c *Config) {\n\t\t\t\tbruteForce, ok := c.Options[\"brute_force\"].(map[string]interface{})\n\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"BruteForce not found\")\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tenabled, ok := bruteForce[\"enabled\"].(bool)\n\t\t\t\tif !ok || enabled {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"BruteForce should not be enabled\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"failure - missing wordlist\",\n\t\t\targs: args{cfg: []byte(`\noptions:\n  brute_force:\n    enabled: true`)},\n\t\t\twantErr: false,\n\t\t\tassertionFunc: func(t *testing.T, c *Config) {\n\t\t\t\tbruteForce, ok := c.Options[\"brute_force\"].(map[string]interface{})\n\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"BruteForce not found\")\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t_, ok = bruteForce[\"wordlists\"].([]interface{})\n\t\t\t\tif ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Wordlist was found, but it is not in the YAML\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"success - minimal wordlist\",\n\t\t\targs: args{cfg: []byte(`\noptions:\n  brute_force:\n    enabled: true\n    wordlists: \n      - \"wordlist_file\"`)},\n\t\t\twantErr: false,\n\t\t\tassertionFunc: func(t *testing.T, c *Config) {\n\t\t\t\tbruteForce, ok := c.Options[\"brute_force\"].(map[string]interface{})\n\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"BruteForce not found\")\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\twordlist, ok := bruteForce[\"wordlists\"].([]interface{})\n\t\t\t\tif !ok || len(wordlist) != 1 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Wordlist not correctly set\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tc := &Config{}\n\t\t\tif err := yaml.Unmarshal(tt.args.cfg, c); (err != nil) != tt.wantErr {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"yaml.Unmarshal() error = %v, wantErr %v\", err, tt.wantErr)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttt.assertionFunc(t, c)\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestConfigLoadAlterationSettings(t *testing.T) {\n\ttype args struct {\n\t\tcfg []byte\n\t}\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname          string\n\t\targs          args\n\t\twantErr       bool\n\t\tassertionFunc func(*testing.T, *Config)\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"success - check default for enabled\",\n\t\t\targs: args{cfg: []byte(`\noptions:\n  name_alteration:\n    enabled: false\n    wordlists: []\n            `)},\n\t\t\twantErr: false,\n\t\t\tassertionFunc: func(t *testing.T, c *Config) {\n\t\t\t\tnameAlteration, ok := c.Options[\"name_alteration\"].(map[string]interface{})\n\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"NameAlteration not found\")\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tenabled, ok := nameAlteration[\"enabled\"].(bool)\n\t\t\t\tif !ok || enabled {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"NameAlteration should not be enabled\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"success - enabled, with wordlist file\",\n\t\t\targs: args{cfg: []byte(`\noptions:\n  name_alteration:\n    enabled: true\n    wordlists: \n      - \"test_word\"\n            `)},\n\t\t\twantErr: false,\n\t\t\tassertionFunc: func(t *testing.T, c *Config) {\n\t\t\t\tnameAlteration, ok := c.Options[\"name_alteration\"].(map[string]interface{})\n\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"NameAlteration not found\")\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tenabled, ok := nameAlteration[\"enabled\"].(bool)\n\t\t\t\tif !ok || !enabled {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"NameAlteration not enabled\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\twordlist, ok := nameAlteration[\"wordlists\"].([]interface{})\n\t\t\t\tif !ok || len(wordlist) != 1 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Wordlist not correctly set\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"failure - All rights reserved.\n// Use of this source code is governed by Apache 2 LICENSE that can be found in the LICENSE file.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\npackage config\n\nimport (\n\t\"bufio\"\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"compress/gzip\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"math/rand\"\n\t\"net\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"path/filepath\"\n\t\"regexp\"\n\t\"runtime\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/caffix/stringset\"\n\t\"github.com/google/uuid\"\n\t\"gopkg.in/yaml.v3\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\toutputDirName  = \"amass\"\n\tdefaultCfgFile = \"config.yaml\"\n\tcfgEnvironVar  = \"AMASS_CONFIG\"\n\tsystemCfgDir   = \"/etc\"\n)\n\n// Updater allows an object to implement a method that updates a configuration.\ntype Updater interface {\n\tOverrideConfig(*Config) error\n}\n\n// Config passes along Amass configuration settings and options.\ntype Config struct {\n\tsync.Mutex `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// A Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) for the enumeration\n\tUUID uuid.UUID `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// The pseudo-random number generator\n\tRand *rand.Rand `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// Logger for error messages\n\tLog *log.Logger `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// The date/time that discoveries must be active since to be included in the findings\n\tCollectionStartTime time.Time `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// Seed struct that contains the provided names and CIDRs\n\tSeed *Scope `yaml:\"seed,omitempty\" json:\"seed,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Scope struct that contains ASN, CIDR, Domain, IP, and ports in scope\n\tScope *Scope `yaml:\"scope,omitempty\" json:\"scope,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Defines options like datasources config path and stuff like that\n\tOptions map[string]interface{} `yaml:\"options,omitempty\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// The global transformation settings\n\tDefaultTransformations *Transformation `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// Filepath of the configuration file. `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// The directory that stores the bolt db and other files created\n\tDir string `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// The graph databases used by the system / enumerations\n\tGraphDBs []*Database `yaml:\"-\" json:\"database,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// The maximum number of concurrent DNS queries\n\tMaxDNSQueries int `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// The list of json:\"brute_force,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Will recursive brute forcing be performed?\n\tRecursive bool `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// Minimum number of subdomain discoveries before performing recursive brute forcing\n\tMinForRecursive int `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// Maximum depth for bruteforcing\n\tMaxDepth int `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// Will discovered subdomain name alterations be generated?\n\tAlterations    bool     `yaml:\"-\" json:\"alterations,omitempty\"`\n\tFlipWords      bool     `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\tFlipNumbers    bool     `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\tAddWords       bool     `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\tAddNumbers     bool     `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\tMinForWordFlip int      `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\tEditDistance   int      `yaml:\"-\" Determines if zone transfers will be attempted\n\tActive bool `yaml:\"active,omitempty\" json:\"active,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Determines rigidness of the enumeration\n\tRigid bool `yaml:\"rigid_boundaries\" json:\"rigid_boundaries\"`\n\n\tblacklistLock sync.Mutex `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// A list of data sources that should not be utilized\n\tSourceFilter struct json:\"domains,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// IP Net.IP\n\tAddresses []net.IP `yaml:\"-\" json:\"ips,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// The IP addresses specified as in scope\n\tIP []string `yaml:\"ips,omitempty\" []interface{} `yaml:\"ports,omitempty\" json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// The ports checked for certificates\n\tPorts []int `yaml:\"-\" json:\"ports,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// A blacklist of subdomain names that will not be investigated\n\tBlacklist []string `yaml:\"blacklist,omitempty\" json:\"blacklist,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// NewConfig returns a default configuration object.\nfunc NewConfig() *Config {\n\treturn &Config{\n\t\tUUID:                uuid.New(),\n\t\tRand:                rand.New(rand.NewSource(time.Now().UTC().UnixNano())),\n\t\tLog:                 log.New(io.Discard, \"\", 0),\n\t\tCollectionStartTime: time.Now(),\n\t\tSeed:                &Scope{},\n\t\tScope:               &Scope{Ports: []int{80, 443}},\n\t\tOptions:             make(map[string]interface{}),\n\t\tMinForRecursive:     1,\n\t\tFlipWords:           true,\n\t\tFlipNumbers:         true,\n\t\tAddWords:            true,\n\t\tAddNumbers:          true,\n\t\tMinForWordFlip:      2,\n\t\tEditDistance:        1,\n\t\tRecursive:           true,\n\t\tMinimumTTL:          1440,\n\t\tResolversQPS:        DefaultQueriesPerPublicResolver,\n\t\tTrustedQPS:          DefaultQueriesPerBaselineResolver,\n\t\tDataSrcConfigs: &DataSourceConfig{\n\t\t\tGlobalOptions: make(map[string]int),\n\t\t},\n\t\tTransformations: make(map[string]*Transformation),\n\t\tDefaultTransformations: &Transformation{\n\t\t\tTTL:        1440,\n\t\t\tConfidence: 50,\n\t\t\tPriority:   5,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// UpdateConfig allows the provided Updater to update the current configuration.\nfunc (c *Config) UpdateConfig(update Updater) error {\n\treturn update.OverrideConfig(c)\n}\n\n// CheckSettings runs some sanity checks on the configuration options selected.\nfunc (c *Config) CheckSettings() error {\n\tvar err error\n\n\tif c.BruteForcing {\n\t\tif c.Passive {\n\t\t\treturn errors.New(\"brute forcing cannot be performed without DNS resolution\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif c.Passive && c.Active {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"active enumeration cannot be performed without DNS resolution\")\n\t}\n\n\tc.Wordlist, err = ExpandMaskWordlist(c.Wordlist)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tc.AltWordlist, err = ExpandMaskWordlist(c.AltWordlist)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\treturn err\n}\n\n// LoadSettings parses settings from an .yaml file and assigns them to the Config.\nfunc (c *Config) LoadSettings(path string) error {\n\t// Determine and store the absolute path of the config file\n\tabsolutePath, err := filepath.Abs(path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t_ = c.LoadDatabaseEnvSettings()\n\t\t_ = c.LoadEngineEnvSettings()\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get absolute path of the configuration file: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tc.Filepath = absolutePath\n\n\t// Open the configuration file\n\tdata, err := os.ReadFile(c.Filepath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t_ = c.LoadDatabaseEnvSettings()\n\t\t_ = c.LoadEngineEnvSettings()\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to load the main configuration file: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\terr = yaml.Unmarshal(data, c)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t_ = c.LoadDatabaseEnvSettings()\n\t\t_ = c.LoadEngineEnvSettings()\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error mapping configuration settings to internal values: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif err := c.loadSeedandScopeSettings(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tloads := []func(cfg *Config) error{\n\t\tc.loadAlterationSettings,\n\t\tc.loadBruteForceSettings,\n\t\tc.loadDatabaseSettings,\n\t\tc.loadDataSourceSettings,\n\t\tc.loadResolverSettings,\n\t\tc.loadTransformSettings,\n\t\tc.loadEngineSettings,\n\t\tc.loadActiveSettings,\n\t\tc.loadRigidSettings,\n\t}\n\tfor _, load := range loads {\n\t\tif err := load(c); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// AbsPathFromConfigDir Creates a file path that is relative the the configuration file location.\n// If the path is already absolute, return it as is.\nfunc (c *Config) AbsPathFromConfigDir(path string) (string, error) {\n\t// If the path is already absolute, return it as is\n\tif filepath.IsAbs(path) {\n\t\t// Check if the file exists\n\t\tif _, err := os.Stat(path); os.IsNotExist(err) {\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"file does not exist: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn path, nil\n\t}\n\t// Get the directory of the current config file\n\tcfgDir := filepath.Dir(c.Filepath)\n\t// Clean the incoming path to ensure it doesn't have any problematic elements\n\tcleanPath := filepath.Clean(path)\n\t// Construct the absolute path by joining the config directory and the relative path\n\tabsPath := filepath.Join(cfgDir, cleanPath)\n\t// Check if the file exists\n\tif _, err := os.Stat(absPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"file does not exist: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn absPath, nil\n}\nfunc (s *Scope) toCIDRs(strings []string) []*net.IPNet {\n\tcidrs := make([]*net.IPNet, len(strings))\n\tfor i, str := range strings {\n\t\t_, cidr, _ := net.ParseCIDR(str)\n\t\tcidrs[i] = cidr\n\t}\n\treturn cidrs\n}\n\n// AcquireConfig populates the Config struct provided by the Config argument.\nfunc AcquireConfig(dir, file string, cfg *Config) error {\n\tvar path, dircfg, syscfg string\n\n\tcfg.Dir = OutputDirectory(dir)\n\tif finfo, err := os.Stat(cfg.Dir); cfg.Dir != \"\" && !os.IsNotExist(err) && finfo.IsDir() {\n\t\tdircfg = filepath.Join(cfg.Dir, defaultCfgFile)\n\t}\n\n\tif runtime.GOOS != \"windows\" {\n\t\tsyscfg = filepath.Join(filepath.Join(systemCfgDir, outputDirName), defaultCfgFile)\n\t}\n\n\tif file != \"\" {\n\t\tpath = file\n\t} else if f, set := os.LookupEnv(cfgEnvironVar); set {\n\t\tpath = f\n\t} else if _, err := os.Stat(dircfg); err == nil {\n\t\tpath = dircfg\n\t} else if _, err := os.Stat(syscfg); err == nil {\n\t\tpath = syscfg\n\t}\n\n\treturn cfg.LoadSettings(path)\n}\n\n// OutputDirectory returns the file path of the Amass output directory. A suitable\n// path provided will be used as the output directory instead.\nfunc OutputDirectory(dir ...string) string {\n\tif len(dir) > 0 && dir[0] != \"\" {\n\t\treturn dir[0]\n\t}\n\n\tif path, err := os.UserConfigDir(); err == nil {\n\t\treturn filepath.Join(path, outputDirName)\n\t}\n\n\treturn \"\"\n}\n\n// GetListFromFile reads a wordlist text or gzip file and returns the slice of words.\nfunc GetListFromFile(path string) ([]string, error) {\n\tvar reader io.Reader\n\n\tabsPath, err := filepath.Abs(path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get absolute path: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tfile, err := os.Open(absPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error opening the file %s: %v\", absPath, err)\n\t}\n\tdefer func() { _ = file.Close() }()\n\treader = file\n\n\t// We need to determine if this is a gzipped file or a plain text file, so we\n\t// first read the first 512 bytes to pass them down to http.DetectContentType\n\t// for mime detection. The file is rewinded before passing it along to the\n\t// next reader\n\thead := make([]byte, 512)\n\tif _, err = file.Read(head); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error reading the first 512 bytes from %s: %s\", absPath, err)\n\t}\n\tif _, err = file.Seek(0, 0); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error rewinding the file %s: %s\", absPath, err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Read the file as gzip if it's actually compressed\n\tif mt := http.DetectContentType(head); mt == \"application/gzip\" || mt == \"application/x-gzip\" {\n\t\tgzReader, err := gzip.NewReader(file)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error gz-reading the file %s: %v\", absPath, err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer func() { _ = gzReader.Close() }()\n\t\treader = gzReader\n\t}\n\n\ts, err := getWordList(reader)\n\treturn s, err\n}\n\nfunc getWordList(reader io.Reader) ([]string, error) {\n\tvar words []string\n\n\tscanner := bufio.NewScanner(reader)\n\tfor scanner.Scan() {\n\t\t// Get the next word in the list\n\t\tw := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())\n\t\tif err := scanner.Err(); err == nil && w != \"\" {\n\t\t\twords = append(words, w)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn stringset.Deduplicate(words), nil\n}\n\n// JSON returns the JSON encoding of the configuration without escaping HTML characters.\nfunc (c *Config) JSON() ([]byte, error) {\n\ttype Alias Config\n\tbuffer := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tencoder := json.NewEncoder(buffer)\n\tencoder.SetEscapeHTML(false)\n\n\terr := encoder.Encode(&struct{ *Alias }{Alias: (*Alias)(c)})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn buffer.Bytes(), nil\n}\n"

\"google.com\", \"yahoo.com\"}\n\tc.AddDomains(list...)\n\tgot := c.Domains()\n\tsort.Strings(list)\n\tsort.Strings(got)\n\tc.AddDomains(list...)\n\n\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(list, got) got)\n\t\t}\n\t\tt.Run(\"Testing Domains...\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tif c.Domains() == nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"No domains in current configuration.\")\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif len(c.Domains()) <=         "content": "// Copyright © by Jeff Foley 2017-2025. All rights reserved.\n// Use of this source code is governed by Apache 2 LICENSE that can be found in the LICENSE file.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\npackage config\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\t\"gopkg.in/yaml.v3\"\n)\n\n// DataSource contains the configurations specific to a data source.\ntype DataSource struct {\n\tName  string                  `yaml:\"name,omitempty\" json:\"name,omitempty\"`\n\tTTL   int                     `yaml:\"ttl,omitempty\" json:\"ttl,omitempty\"`\n\tCreds map[string]*Credentials `yaml:\"creds,omitempty\" json:\"creds,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Credentials contains values required for authenticating with web APIs.\ntype Credentials struct {\n\tName     string `yaml:\"-\" json:\"-\"`\n\tUsername string `yaml:\"username,omitempty\" json:\"username,omitempty\"`\n\tPassword string `yaml:\"password,omitempty\" json:\"password,omitempty\"`\n\tApikey   string `yaml:\"apikey,omitempty\" json:\"apikey,omitempty\"`\n\tSecret   string `yaml:\"secret,omitempty\" json:\"secret,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// DataSourceConfig contains the configuration for multiple data sources.\ntype DataSourceConfig struct {\n\tDatasources   []*DataSource  `yaml:\"datasources,omitempty\" json:\"datasources,omitempty\"`\n\tGlobalOptions map[string]int `yaml:\"global_options,omitempty\" json:\"global_options,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// GetDataSourceConfig returns the DataSourceConfig associated with the data source name argument.\nfunc (c *Config) GetDataSourceConfig(source string) *DataSource {\n\tc.Lock()\n\tdefer c.Unlock()\n\n\tkey := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(source))\n\tif key == \"\" || c.DataSrcConfigs == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar dsc *DataSource\n\tfor _, src := range c.DataSrcConfigs.Datasources {\n\t\tif strings.ToLower(src.Name) == key {\n\t\t\tdsc = src\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn dsc\n}\n\n// AddCredentials adds the Credentials provided to the configuration.\nfunc (ds *DataSource) AddCredentials(accountName string, cred *Credentials) error {\n\tif accountName == \"\" || ds == nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"AddCredentials: The accountName argument is invalid\")\n\t}\n\n\tif ds.Creds == nil {\n\t\tds.Creds = make(map[string]*Credentials)\n\t}\n\n\tds.Creds[accountName] = cred\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// GetCredentials returns the first set of Credentials associated with the given DataSource name.\nfunc (dsc *DataSourceConfig) GetCredentials(dsName string) *Credentials {\n\tif dsc == nil || dsc.Datasources == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, src := range dsc.Datasources {\n\t\tif src.Name == dsName && src.Creds != nil {\n\t\t\tfor _, creds := range src.Creds {\n\t\t\t\treturn creds // Return the first set of credentials found\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Config) loadDataSourceSettings(cfg *Config) error {\n\t// Retrieve the datasources file path from the options\n\tpathInterface, ok := c.Options[\"datasources\"]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\t// \"datasources\" not found in options, so nothing to do here.\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tpath, ok := pathInterface.(string)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"datasources option is not a string\")\n\t}\n\t// Construct the absolute path by joining the current working directory and the relative path\n\tabsPath, err := c.AbsPathFromConfigDir(path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get absolute path: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\t// Load the datasources YAML file\n\tdata, err := os.ReadFile(absPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error reading datasources file: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\t// Unmarshal the YAML data into a DataSourceConfig\n\tvar dsConfig DataSourceConfig\n\terr = yaml.Unmarshal(data, &dsConfig)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error unmarshalling datasources YAML: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tdsConfig.MapNames()\n\t// Assign the unmarshalled DataSourceConfig to the Config struct\n\tc.DataSrcConfigs = &dsConfig\n\tc.DataSrcConfigs.ttlCheck()\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (dsc *DataSourceConfig) ttlCheck() {\n\t// The global minimum TTL is already loaded during the YAML unmarshalling process\n\tfor _, ds := range dsc.Datasources {\n\t\t// Ensure the TTL is not less than the global minimum\n\t\tif dsc.GlobalOptions != nil {\n\t\t\tif minTTL, ok := dsc.GlobalOptions[\"minimum_ttl\"]; ok && minTTL > ds.TTL {\n\t\t\t\tds.TTL = dsc.GlobalOptions[\"minimum_ttl\"]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// MapNames assigns the name of the DataSource to each associated Credential's Name field.\n// This is especially useful after unmarshalling data where the relationship between a DataSource and its\n// credentials may not have been explicitly set in the source data.\nfunc (dsc *DataSourceConfig) MapNames() {\n\t// Assign the DataSource name to each Credential's Name field in the Datasource\n\tfor _, src := range dsc.Datasources {\n\t\tif src.Creds == nil {\n\t\t\tsrc.Creds = make(map[string]*Credentials)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor accountName, creds := range src.Creds {\n\t\t\tcreds.Name = src.Name\n\t\t\tsrc.Creds[accountName] = creds\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n"
      },

All rights reserved.\n// Use of this source code is governed by Apache 2 LICENSE that can be found in the LICENSE file.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\npackage config\n\nimport (\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"gopkg.in/yaml.v3\"\n)\n\nfunc TestGetDataSourceConfig(t *testing.T) {\n\tc := NewConfig()\n\n\t// Scenario 1: Empty DataSourceConfig\n\tc.DataSrcConfigs = &DataSourceConfig{\n\t\tGlobalOptions: make(map[string]int),\n\t}\n\tif dsc := c.GetDataSourceConfig(\"nonexistent\"); dsc != nil {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"GetDataSourceConfig returned a non-nil value when provided an nonexistent argument\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Scenario 2: DataSource with no creds\n\tnameNoCreds := \"testNoCreds\"\n\tc.DataSrcConfigs.Datasources = []*DataSource{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tName: nameNoCreds,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\tif dsc := c.GetDataSourceConfig(nameNoCreds); dsc == nil || dsc.Name != nameWithCreds {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"GetDataSourceConfig returned an error when provided a valid argument with creds\")\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestAddCredentials(t *testing.T) {\n\tname := \"test\"\n\tc := NewConfig()\n\n\t// Add a DataSource to the Config object to use it in the test\n\tc.DataSrcConfigs = &DataSourceConfig{\n\t\tDatasources: []*DataSource{\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tName: name,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tdsc := c.GetDataSourceConfig(name)\n\n\tif err := dsc.AddCredentials(\"account1\", &Credentials{Username: \"username\", Password: \"password\"}); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"AddCredentials returned an error when provided an valid arguments: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif dsc.Creds[\"account1\"].Username != \"username\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"AddCredentials failed to enter the new credentials into the data source configuration\")\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestGetCredentials(t *testing.T) {\n\tc := NewConfig()\n\n\t// Add a DataSource with credentials to the Config object to use it in the test\n\tc.DataSrcConfigs = &DataSourceConfig{\n\t\tDatasources: []*DataSource{\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tName: \"test\",\n\t\t\t\tCreds: map[string]*Credentials{\n\t\t\t\t\t\"account1\": {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tUsername: \"username\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tPassword: \"password\",\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\tdsc := c.DataSrcConfigs\n\n\t// Pass the name of the data source when calling GetCredentials\n\tif creds := dsc.GetCredentials(\"test\"); creds == nil || creds.Username != \"avuser\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to load data source credentials\")\n\t}\n\n\tif c.DataSrcConfigs.GlobalOptions[\"minimum_ttl\"] != 1440 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to load global options\")\n\t}\n}\n"

        "name": "engineapi.go",
        "path": "config/engineapi.go",
        "type": "file",
        "content": "// Copyright © by Jeff Foley 2017-2025. All rights reserved.\n// Use of this source code is governed by Apache 2 LICENSE that can be found in the LICENSE file.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\npackage config\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/url\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// EngAPI structure holds various components necessary for establishing a connection with an Engine API.\n// It includes fields for the scheme (http or https), the full API URL, authentication credentials (username, password),\n// host and port information, the specific path (name) of the API, and any extra options that may be used during the connection.\ntype EngAPI struct {\n\tScheme   string // Engine API scheme (http, https)\n\tURL      string // Full URI to the Engine API\n\tUsername string // Username for authentication\n\tPassword string // Password for authentication\n\tHost     string // Host of the Engine API\n\tPort     string // Port of the Engine API\n\tPath     string // Name of the Engine API\n\tOptions  string // Extra options used while connecting to the Engine API\n}\n\nconst (\n\tengineUser   = \"AMASS_ENGINE_USER\"\n\tenginePass   = \"AMASS_ENGINE_PASSWORD\"\n\tengineHost   = \"AMASS_ENGINE_HOST\"\n\tenginePort   = \"AMASS_ENGINE_PORT\"\n\tenginePath   = \"AMASS_ENGINE_PATH\"\n\tengineScheme = \"AMASS_ENGINE_SCHEME\"\n)\n\n// loadEngineSettings is responsible for extracting the \"engine\" URL from the application's configuration\n// and initializing the EngAPI structure. It performs several checks such as ensuring the configuration options\n// are initialized and the \"engine\" key is present and of type string. If any of these checks fail, an error is returned.\nfunc (c *Config) loadEngineSettings(cfg *Config) error {\n\t// Check if configuration options are initialized; if not, return an error.\n\tif c.Options == nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"config options are not initialized\")\n\t}\n\t// Attempt to retrieve the \"engine\" value from the configuration options.\n\tapiURIInterface, ok := c.Options[\"engine\"]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn c.LoadEngineEnvSettings()\n\t}\n\t// Assert that the \"engine\" option is of type string; if not, return an error specifying the incorrect type received.\n\tapiURI, ok := apiURIInterface.(string)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected 'engine' to be a string, got %T\", apiURIInterface)\n\t}\n\t// Load the Engine API URI information into the EngAPI structure.\n\t// If there's an error during this process, it's returned to the calling function.\n\tif err := c.loadEngineURI(apiURI); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// LoadEngineEnvSettings initializes the EngAPI structure with the Environment variables.\nfunc (c *Config) LoadEngineEnvSettings() error {\n\tapiURI := \"\"\n\teng := &EngAPI{}\n\n\th := \"localhost\"\n\tif henv, set := os.LookupEnv(engineHost); set {\n\t\th = henv\n\t}\n\teng.Host = h\n\tu := \"\"\n\tif uEnv, set := os.LookupEnv(engineUser); set {\n\t\teng.Username = uEnv\n\t\tu = uEnv + \"@\"\n\t}\n\tp := \"\"\n\tif penv, set := os.LookupEnv(enginePass); set {\n\t\teng.Password = penv\n\t\tp = \":\" + penv + \"@\"\n\t}\n\tscheme := \"http\"\n\tif s, set := os.LookupEnv(engineScheme); set {\n\t\tscheme = s\n\t}\n\teng.Scheme = scheme\n\tport := \"4000\"\n\tif pEnv, set := os.LookupEnv(enginePort); set {\n\t\tport = pEnv\n\t}\n\teng.Port = port\n\tpath := \"graphql\"\n\tif pEnv, set := os.LookupEnv(enginePath); set {\n\t\tpath = pEnv\n\t}\n\teng.Path = path\n\tif p != \"\" {\n\t\tu = u[:len(u)-1]\n\t}\n\tapiURI = scheme + \"://\" + u + p + h + \":\" + port + \"/\" + path\n\teng.URL = apiURI\n\n\tc.EngineAPI = eng\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// loadEngineURI takes the Engine API's URI as a string, parses it, and populates the EngAPI structure with the URI's components.\n// It performs validations to ensure the URI contains a valid scheme and hostname. If parsing fails or any validation check doesn't pass,\n// an error is returned. It also handles extracting authentication information and any additional options provided in the URI query.\nfunc (c *Config) loadEngineURI(apiURI string) error {\n\t// Parse the raw URI string to a url.URL object. If the URI is malformed, an error is returned.\n\tu, err := url.Parse(apiURI)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\t// Check for valid scheme\n\tif u.Scheme == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"missing scheme in database URI\")\n\t}\n\t// Check for reachable hostname\n\tif u.Hostname() == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"missing hostname in database URI\")\n\t}\n\t// If the path is present in the URI and is more than just a \"/\", it's trimmed and used.\n\t// If the path is empty or just a \"/\", it defaults to an empty string.\n\tapiURIPath := \"\"\n\t// Only get the api file path name if it's not empty or a single slash\n\tif u.Path != \"\" && u.Path != \"/\" {\n\t\tapiURIPath = strings.TrimPrefix(u.Path, \"/\")\n\t}\n\t// Initialize a new EngAPI object with data from the parsed URI.\n\tapi := &EngAPI{\n\t\tURL:      apiURI,\n\t\tScheme:   u.Scheme,\n\t\tUsername: u.User.Username(),\n\t\tPath:     apiURIPath,\n\t\tHost:     u.Hostname(), // Hostname without port\n\t\tPort:     u.Port(),     // Get port\n\t}\n\t// If a password is set in the URI, it's extracted and stored in the EngAPI object.\n\tpassword, isSet := u.User.Password()\n\tif isSet {\n\t\tapi.Password = password\n\t}\n\t// If there are query parameters present in the URI, they are parsed and encoded as a string,\n\t// then stored in the EngAPI object's Options field. If parsing fails, an error is returned.\n\tif u.RawQuery != \"\" {\n\t\tqueryParams, err := url.ParseQuery(u.RawQuery)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to parse engine API URI query parameters: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tapi.Options = queryParams.Encode() // Encode url.Values to a string\n\t}\n\t// The Config's EngineAPI field is set to the newly created EngAPI object.\n\tc.EngineAPI = api\n\treturn nil\n}\n"
      },

All rights reserved.\n// Use of this source code is governed by Apache 2 LICENSE that can be found in the LICENSE file.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\npackage config\n\nimport (\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n\t\"gopkg.in/yaml.v3\"\n)\n\n// Valid mock YAML configuration for testing\nvar validMockConfigYAML = []byte(`\noptions:\n  engine: \"http://username:password@127.0.0.1:80/path?option1=value1\"\n`)\n\n// Invalid mock YAML configuration (non-string engine value)\nvar invalidTypeMockConfigYAML = []byte(`\noptions:\n  engine: 123\n`)\n\n// Invalid mock YAML configuration (malformed YAML)\nvar malformedMockConfigYAML = []byte(`\n\toptions\n  engine \"http://username:password@127.0.0.1:80/path?option1=value1\"\n`)\n\nfunc TestLoadEngineSettings_ValidConfig(t *testing.T) {\n\tc := NewConfig()\n\n\terr := yaml.Unmarshal(validMockConfigYAML, &c)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Unmarshalling valid YAML should not produce an error\")\n\n\terr = c.loadEngineSettings(c)\n\tassert.NoError(t, err, \"loadEngineSettings should not return an error with valid config\")\n\tassert.NotNil(t, c.EngineAPI, \"EngineAPI should not be nil after loading settings\")\n}\n\nfunc TestLoadEngineSettings_InvalidType(t *testing.T) {\n\tc := NewConfig()\n\n\terr := yaml.Unmarshal(invalidTypeMockConfigYAML, &c)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Unmarshalling invalid type YAML should not produce an error\")\n\n\terr = c.loadEngineSettings(c)\n\tassert.Error(t, err, \"loadEngineSettings should return an error if 'engine' type is not string\")\n}\n\nfunc TestLoadEngineSettings_MalformedYAML(t *testing.T) {\n\tc := NewConfig()\n\n\terr := yaml.Unmarshal(malformedMockConfigYAML, &c)\n\tassert.Error(t, err, \"Unmarshalling malformed YAML should produce an error\")\n}\n\nfunc TestLoadEngineURI_ValidURI(t *testing.T) {\n\tc := NewConfig()\n\n\terr := yaml.Unmarshal(validMockConfigYAML, &c)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Unmarshalling valid YAML should not produce an error\")\n\n\t_ = c.loadEngineSettings(c)\n\n\tassert.Equal(t, \"http\", c.EngineAPI.Scheme, \"Scheme should be 'http'\")\n\tassert.Equal(t, \"username\", c.EngineAPI.Username, \"Username should be 'username'\")\n\tassert.Equal(t, \"password\", c.EngineAPI.Password, \"Password should be 'password'\")\n\tassert.Equal(t, \"127.0.0.1\", c.EngineAPI.Host, \"Host should be 'hostname'\")\n\tassert.Equal(t, \"80\", c.EngineAPI.Port, \"Port should be 'port'\")\n\tassert.Equal(t, \"path\", c.EngineAPI.Path, \"Path should be 'path'\")\n\tassert.Equal(t, \"option1=value1\", c.EngineAPI.Options, \"Options should be 'option1=value1'\")\n}\n\nfunc TestLoadEngineURI_InvalidURI(t *testing.T) {\n\tc := NewConfig()\n\n\t// Set 'engine' option to an invalid URI\n\tc.Options[\"engine\"] = \"http:://invalid-uri\"\n\n\terr := c.loadEngineSettings(c)\n\tassert.Error(t, err, \"loadEngineSettings should return an error for invalid URI\")\n}\n\nfunc TestLoadEngineURI_MissingScheme(t *testing.T) {\n\tc := NewConfig()\n\n\t// Set 'engine' option to a URI missing a scheme\n\tc.Options[\"engine\"] = \"username:password@hostname:port/path?option1=value1\"\n\n\terr := c.loadEngineSettings(c)\n\tassert.Error(t, err, \"loadEngineSettings should return an error for missing scheme in URI\")\n}\nfunc TestLoadEngineEnvSettings_ValidEnvSettings(t *testing.T) {\n\tc := NewConfig()\n\n\t// Set the required environment variables\n\t_ = os.Setenv(engineHost, \"127.0.0.1\")\n\t_ = os.Setenv(engineUser, \"username\")\n\t_ = os.Setenv(enginePass, \"password\")\n\t_ = os.Setenv(engineScheme, \"http\")\n\t_ = os.Setenv(enginePort, \"80\")\n\t_ = os.Setenv(enginePath, \"path\")\n\n\terr := c.LoadEngineEnvSettings()\n\tassert.NoError(t, err, \"LoadEngineEnvSettings should not return an error with valid environment settings\")\n\tassert.NotNil(t, c.EngineAPI, \"EngineAPI should not be nil after loading environment settings\")\n\n\t// Verify the loaded EngineAPI values\n\tassert.Equal(t, \"http\", c.EngineAPI.Scheme, \"Scheme should be 'http'\")\n\tassert.Equal(t, \"username\", c.EngineAPI.Username, \"Username should be 'username'\")\n\tassert.Equal(t, \"password\", c.EngineAPI.Password, \"Password should be 'password'\")\n\tassert.Equal(t, \"127.0.0.1\", c.EngineAPI.Host, \"Host should be '127.0.0.1'\")\n\tassert.Equal(t, \"80\", c.EngineAPI.Port, \"Port should be '80'\")\n\tassert.Equal(t, \"path\", c.EngineAPI.Path, \"Path should be 'path'\")\n}\n\nfunc TestLoadEngineEnvSettings_MissingEnvSettings(t *testing.T) {\n\tc := NewConfig()\n\n\t// Unset the required environment variables\n\t_ = os.Unsetenv(engineHost)\n\t_ = os.Unsetenv(engineUser)\n\t_ = os.Unsetenv(enginePass)\n\t_ = os.Unsetenv(engineScheme)\n\t_ = os.Unsetenv(enginePort)\n\t_ = os.Unsetenv(enginePath)\n\n\terr := c.LoadEngineEnvSettings()\n\t_ = c.loadEngineURI(c.EngineAPI.URL)\n\tassert.Nil(t, err, \"LoadEngineEnvSettings shouldn't return an error since default values are in place\")\n\tassert.NotEmpty(t, c.EngineAPI, \"EngineAPI should be properly populated\")\n}\n"

        "name": "graphdb.go",
        "path": "config/graphdb.go",
        "type": "file",
        "content": "// Copyright © by Jeff Foley 2017-2025. All rights reserved.\n// Use of this source code is governed by Apache 2 LICENSE that can be found in the LICENSE file.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\npackage config\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/url\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// Database contains values required for connecting with graph database.\ntype Database struct {\n\tSystem   string `json:\"system,omitempty\"`   // Database system type (Postgres, MySQL, etc.)\n\tPrimary  bool   `json:\"primary,omitempty\"`  // Whether this database is the primary store\n\tURL      string `json:\"url,omitempty\"`      // Full URI to the database\n\tUsername string `json:\"username,omitempty\"` // Username for authentication\n\tPassword string `json:\"password,omitempty\"` // Password for authentication\n\tHost     string `json:\"host,omitempty\"`     // Host of the database\n\tPort     string `json:\"port,omitempty\"`     // Port of the database\n\tDBName   string `json:\"db_name,omitempty\"`  // Name of the database\n\tOptions  string `json:\"options,omitempty\"`  // Extra options used while connecting to the database\n}\n\nconst (\n\tamassUser   = \"AMASS_DB_USER\"\n\tamassPass   = \"AMASS_DB_PASSWORD\"\n\tassetDB     = \"AMASS_DB_HOST\"\n\tassetPort   = \"AMASS_DB_PORT\"\n\tassetDBName = \"AMASS_DB_NAME\"\n)\n\nfunc (c *Config) loadDatabaseSettings(cfg *Config) error {\n\tif c.Options == nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"config options are not initialized\")\n\t}\n\n\tdbURIInterface, ok := c.Options[\"database\"]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\tif err := c.LoadDatabaseEnvSettings(); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdbURI, ok := dbURIInterface.(string)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected 'database' to be a string, got %T\", dbURIInterface)\n\t}\n\n\tif _, err := url.Parse(dbURI); err == nil {\n\t\tif err := c.loadDatabase(dbURI); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// LoadDatabaseEnvSettings initializes the DB structure with the Environment variables.\nfunc (c *Config) LoadDatabaseEnvSettings() error {\n\tdbURI := \"\"\n\tdb := &Database{\n\t\tPrimary: true,\n\t\tSystem:  \"postgres\",\n\t}\n\n\tvar u string\n\tif uenv, set := os.LookupEnv(amassUser); set {\n\t\tu = uenv\n\t} else {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"environment variable %s is not set\", amassUser)\n\t}\n\tdb.Username = u\n\th := \"localhost\"\n\tif dbEnv, set := os.LookupEnv(assetDB); set {\n\t\th = dbEnv\n\t}\n\tdb.Host = h\n\tport := \"5432\"\n\tif pEnv, set := os.LookupEnv(assetPort); set {\n\t\tport = pEnv\n\t}\n\tdb.Port = port\n\tn := \"assetdb\"\n\tif nEnv, set := os.LookupEnv(assetDBName); set {\n\t\tn = nEnv\n\t}\n\tdb.DBName = n\n\tif p, set := os.LookupEnv(amassPass); set {\n\t\tdb.Password = p\n\t\tdbURI = \"postgres://\" + u + \":\" + p + \"@\" + h + \":\" + port + \"/\" + n\n\t} else {\n\t\tdbURI = \"postgres://\" + u + \"@\" + h + \":\" + port + \"/\" + n\n\t}\n\tdb.URL = dbURI\n\tif c.GraphDBs == nil {\n\t\tc.GraphDBs = make([]*Database, 0)\n\t}\n\tc.GraphDBs = append(c.GraphDBs, db)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Config) loadDatabase(dbURI string) error {\n\tu, err := url.Parse(dbURI)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\t// Check for valid scheme (database type)\n\tif u.Scheme == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"missing scheme in database URI\")\n\t}\n\t// Check for non-empty username\n\tif u.User == nil || u.User.Username() == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"missing username in database URI\")\n\t}\n\t// Check for reachable hostname\n\tif u.Hostname() == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"missing hostname in database URI\")\n\t}\n\n\tdbName := \"\"\n\t// Only get the database name if it's not empty or a single slash\n\tif u.Path != \"\" && u.Path != \"/\" {\n\t\tdbName = strings.TrimPrefix(u.Path, \"/\")\n\t}\n\n\tdb := &Database{\n\t\tPrimary:  true, // Set as primary, because it wouldn't be there otherwise.\n\t\tURL:      dbURI,\n\t\tSystem:   u.Scheme,\n\t\tUsername: u.User.Username(),\n\t\tDBName:   dbName,\n\t\tHost:     u.Hostname(), // Hostname without port\n\t\tPort:     u.Port(),     // Get port\n\t}\n\n\tpassword, isSet := u.User.Password()\n\tif isSet {\n\t\tdb.Password = password\n\t}\n\n\tif u.RawQuery != \"\" {\n\t\tqueryParams, err := url.ParseQuery(u.RawQuery)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to parse database URI query parameters: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdb.Options = queryParams.Encode() // Encode url.Values to a string\n\t}\n\n\tif c.GraphDBs == nil {\n\t\tc.GraphDBs = make([]*Database, 0)\n\t}\n\tc.GraphDBs = append(c.GraphDBs, db)\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// LocalDatabaseSettings returns the Database for the local bolt store.\nfunc (c *Config) LocalDatabaseSettings(dbs []*Database) *Database {\n\tbolt := &Database{\n\t\tSystem:  \"local\",\n\t\tPrimary: true,\n\t\tURL:     OutputDirectory(c.Dir),\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, db := range dbs {\n\t\tif db != nil && db.Primary {\n\t\t\tbolt.Primary = false\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn bolt\n}\n"
      },

\"username\" || db.Password != \"password\" || db.System != \"postgres\" ||\n\t\t\tdb.URL != \"postgres://username:password@localhost:5432/database?sslmode=disable\" || db.DBName != \"database\" ||         "name": "resolvers.go",
        "path": "config/resolvers.go",
        "type": "file",
        "content": "// Copyright © by Jeff Foley 2017-2025. All rights reserved.\n// Use of this source code is governed by Apache 2 LICENSE that can be found in the LICENSE file.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\npackage config\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/csv\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"path/filepath\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\t\"github.com/caffix/stringset\"\n\t\"github.com/owasp-amass/amass/v5/internal/net/http\"\n)\n\n// DefaultQueriesPerPublicResolver is the number of queries sent to each public DNS resolver per second.\nconst DefaultQueriesPerPublicResolver = 5\n\n// DefaultQueriesPerBaselineResolver is the number of queries sent to each trusted DNS resolver per second.\nconst DefaultQueriesPerBaselineResolver = 15\nconst minResolverReliability = 0.85\n\n// DefaultBaselineResolvers is a list of trusted public DNS resolvers.\nvar DefaultBaselineResolvers = []string{\n\t\"8.8.8.8\",        // Google\n\t\"1.1.1.1\",        // Cloudflare\n\t\"9.9.9.9\",        // Quad9\n\t\"208.67.222.222\", // Cisco OpenDNS\n\t\"84.200.69.80\",   // DNS.WATCH\n\t\"64.6.64.6\",      // Neustar DNS\n\t\"8.26.56.26\",     // Comodo Secure DNS\n\t\"205.171.3.65\",   // Level3\n\t\"134.195.4.2\",    // OpenNIC\n\t\"185.228.168.9\",  // CleanBrowsing\n\t\"76.76.19.19\",    // Alternate DNS\n\t\"37.235.1.177\",   // FreeDNS\n\t\"77.88.8.1\",      // Yandex.DNS\n\t\"94.140.14.140\",  // AdGuard\n\t\"38.132.106.139\", // CyberGhost\n\t\"74.82.42.42\",    // Hurricane Electric\n\t\"76.76.2.0\",      // ControlD\n}\n\n// PublicResolvers includes the addresses of public resolvers obtained dynamically.\nvar PublicResolvers []string\n\n// GetPublicDNSResolvers obtains the public DNS server addresses from public-dns.info and assigns them to PublicResolvers.\nfunc GetPublicDNSResolvers() error {\n\turl := \"https://public-dns.info/nameservers-all.csv\"\n\tresp, err := http.RequestWebPage(context.Background(), &http.Request{URL: url})\n\tif err != nil || resp.StatusCode < 200 || resp.StatusCode >= 400 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to obtain the Public DNS csv file at %s: %v\", url, err)\n\t}\n\n\tvar resolvers []string\n\tvar ipIdx, reliabilityIdx int\n\tr := csv.NewReader(strings.NewReader(resp.Body))\n\tfor i := 0; ; i++ {\n\t\trecord, err := r.Read()\n\t\tif err == io.EOF {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif i == 0 {\n\t\t\tfor idx, val := range record {\n\t\t\t\tswitch val {\n\t\t\t\tcase \"ip_address\":\n\t\t\t\t\tipIdx = idx\n\t\t\t\tcase \"reliability\":\n\t\t\t\t\treliabilityIdx = idx\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif rel, err := strconv.ParseFloat(record[reliabilityIdx], 64); err == nil && rel >= minResolverReliability {\n\t\t\tresolvers = append(resolvers, record[ipIdx])\n\t\t}\n\t}\nloop:\n\tfor _, addr := range resolvers {\n\t\tfor _, br := range DefaultBaselineResolvers {\n\t\t\tif addr == br {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue loop\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tPublicResolvers = append(PublicResolvers, addr)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// SetResolvers assigns the untrusted resolver names provided in the parameter to the list in the configuration.\nfunc (c *Config) SetResolvers(resolvers ...string) {\n\tc.Resolvers = []string{}\n\tc.AddResolvers(resolvers...)\n}\n\n// AddResolvers appends the untrusted resolver names provided in the parameter to the list in the configuration.\nfunc (c *Config) AddResolvers(resolvers ...string) {\n\tfor _, r := range resolvers {\n\t\tc.AddResolver(r)\n\t}\n\tc.CalcMaxQPS()\n}\n\n// AddResolver appends the untrusted resolver name provided in the parameter to the list in the configuration.\nfunc (c *Config) AddResolver(resolver string) {\n\tc.Lock()\n\tdefer c.Unlock()\n\n\t// Check that the domain string is not empty\n\tr := strings.TrimSpace(resolver)\n\tif r == \"\" {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tc.Resolvers = stringset.Deduplicate(append(c.Resolvers, resolver))\n}\n\n// SetTrustedResolvers assigns the trusted resolver names provided in the parameter to the list in the configuration.\nfunc (c *Config) SetTrustedResolvers(resolvers ...string) {\n\tc.Resolvers = []string{}\n\tc.AddResolvers(resolvers...)\n}\n\n// AddTrustedResolvers appends the trusted resolver names provided in the parameter to the list in the configuration.\nfunc (c *Config) AddTrustedResolvers(resolvers ...string) {\n\tfor _, r := range resolvers {\n\t\tc.AddTrustedResolver(r)\n\t}\n\tc.CalcMaxQPS()\n}\n\n// AddTrustedResolver appends the trusted resolver name provided in the parameter to the list in the configuration.\nfunc (c *Config) AddTrustedResolver(resolver string) {\n\tc.Lock()\n\tdefer c.Unlock()\n\n\t// Check that the domain string is not empty\n\tr := strings.TrimSpace(resolver)\n\tif r == \"\" {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tc.TrustedResolvers = stringset.Deduplicate(append(c.TrustedResolvers, resolver))\n}\n\n// CalcMaxQPS updates the MaxDNSQueries field of the configuration based on current settings.\nfunc (c *Config) CalcMaxQPS() {\n\tc.MaxDNSQueries = (len(c.Resolvers) * c.ResolversQPS) + (len(c.TrustedResolvers) * c.TrustedQPS)\n}\n\nfunc (c *Config) loadResolverSettings(cfg *Config) error {\n\t// Fetch resolvers from the Options map in the Config.\n\tresolversRaw, ok := c.Options[\"resolvers\"]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\t// \"resolvers\" not found in options, so nothing to do here.\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Type assert the raw resolvers to []interface{}\n\tresolvers, ok := resolversRaw.([]interface{})\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"resolvers section is not a list\")\n\t}\n\n\tvar resolversList []string\n\tfor _, r := range resolvers {\n\t\t// Type assert the resolver to string\n\t\trStr, ok := r.(string)\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"resolver entry %v is not a string\", r)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check if rStr is an IP address.\n\t\tip := net.ParseIP(rStr)\n\t\tif ip != nil {\n\t\t\tresolversList = append(resolversList, rStr)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// rStr is not an IP address, so we assume it is a file path.\n\t\tabsPath, err := c.AbsPathFromConfigDir(rStr)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get absolute path for resolver file: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfileResolvers, err := c.loadResolversFromFile(absPath)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to load resolvers from file: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresolversList = append(resolversList, fileResolvers...)\n\n\t}\n\n\t// Deduplicate the list of resolvers and assign to c.Resolvers.\n\tresolverIPs := stringset.Deduplicate(resolversList)\n\n\tif len(resolverIPs) == 0 {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"no valid resolvers were found\")\n\t}\n\n\tc.Resolvers = resolverIPs\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Config) loadResolversFromFile(path string) ([]string, error) {\n\tabsPath, err := filepath.Abs(path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get absolute path: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tdata, err := os.ReadFile(absPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to open resolvers file: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Split the file data by newlines to get the IP addresses.\n\tlines := strings.Split(string(data), \"\\n\")\n\n\tvar resolvers []string\n\tfor _, line := range lines {\n\t\tline = strings.TrimSpace(line)\n\t\t// Skip empty lines.\n\t\tif line == \"\" {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check if each line in the file is a valid IP address.\n\t\tip := net.ParseIP(line)\n\t\tif ip == nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid IP address in resolvers file: %s\", line)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresolvers = append(resolvers, line)\n\t}\n\n\treturn resolvers, nil\n}\n"
      },
